summary: Normalized legacy M0 qd completion report commit IDs to full SHAs and tightened qd report validation so non-empty commits[] entries must be 40-character lowercase hexadecimal SHAs.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The three legacy M0 completion reports use full commit SHAs.
scripts/qd-reports-check.sh rejects non-empty commits[] entries that are not 40-character lowercase hex SHAs.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: scripts/qd-reports-check.sh validates completion commits[] with test("^[0-9a-f]{40}$"); evidence/m4-qd-report-commit-sha-contract/commands.log records a temporary-copy short-SHA negative smoke test failing as expected.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Existing qd reports pass the stricter check.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-qd-report-commit-sha-contract/commands.log records bash scripts/qd-reports-check.sh and nix develop -c just qd-reports-check passing.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux validation evidence records qd report checking and shared check passing.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-qd-report-commit-sha-contract/commands.log records bash scripts/qd-reports-check.sh, nix develop -c just qd-reports-check, nix develop -c just check, and git diff --check.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No macOS-specific validation is claimed or required.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: realWorldValidation.required is false because this is repository qd report metadata validation only.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Repository qd completion-report metadata validation only; Linux validation passed and macOS validation is not required.
